title: Cheap Houses for Rent in Little Italy
metaTitle: Cheap Houses for Rent in Little Italy, Cleveland
metaDescription: Searching for cheap houses for rent in Little Italy, Cleveland? See rent benchmarks, lower-cost nearby neighborhoods, and how to contact our team.
primaryKeyword: cheap houses for rent Little Italy cleveland
secondaryKeywords: cheap houses near mayfield road cleveland
answerBox: Little Italy's small footprint and closeness to University Circle keep house rents near the district close to Cleveland's citywide averages rather than at the cheap end. Renters on a tight budget typically find lower-cost houses a few miles east, in neighborhoods such as Hough or Glenville, both reachable by the same transit lines.

sections.h2: Is there a cheap house to rent in Little Italy?
body: Little Italy is a small district centered on Mayfield Road, wedged between Lake View Cemetery and University Circle about five miles east of Downtown Cleveland. Its housing stock is dense, older, and limited in supply, and its closeness to Case Western Reserve University, the Cleveland Museum of Art, and the Cleveland Clinic keeps demand — and prices — closer to Cleveland's citywide averages than to the cheap end of the market.

Renters specifically looking for a lower-cost house usually do better widening the search a few miles out. [Tell our leasing team your budget](/p/book-showing) and we'll be upfront about whether anything near Little Italy currently fits, or point you toward comparable, more affordable options nearby.

sections.h2: Cleveland rent benchmarks for budget-minded renters
body: Per Zumper's rent report dated July 4, 2026, Cleveland's overall median rent is **$1,250** a month, up 4.2% year-over-year, and about 36% cheaper than the U.S. national median of $1,950. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report on professionally managed apartments — more common near University Circle than in older neighborhoods — puts the citywide average considerably higher, at **$1,564** for 787 square feet.

Rent Finder Cleveland's own rental homes across Greater Cleveland typically run from about **$700 to $1,800** a month, with most landing around **$1,000** — a range worth using as your baseline when comparing any specific listing near Little Italy.

sections.h2: Lower-cost neighborhoods within reach of Little Italy
body: A few miles from Little Italy, Cleveland's east side has neighborhoods with a larger supply of houses at lower price points, including [Hough](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-hough-cleveland/) between Downtown and University Circle, and Glenville to the northeast near St. Clair Avenue. Both fall within Rent Finder Cleveland's core service area, alongside Fairfax near the Cleveland Clinic's main campus.

items: **Hough:** ZIP 44103, east side, between Downtown and University Circle
**Glenville:** ZIP 44108, northeast side, near St. Clair Avenue
**Fairfax:** ZIP 44104, east side, adjacent to the Cleveland Clinic main campus
**Transit:** HealthLine and RTA bus routes connect all three to University Circle and Downtown

sections.h2: Saving on transportation near Little Italy
body: Little Italy sits directly on two of Cleveland's strongest transit corridors: the **RTA Red Line** heavy rail, serving the University Circle station with a direct line to Downtown's Tower City and Cleveland Hopkins International Airport, and the **HealthLine** bus rapid transit along Euclid Avenue. Renting slightly further out and relying on either line can offset a chunk of what a car costs to own and park in a dense district like this one.

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers as a way to lower monthly cost
body: Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) applicants can apply year-round through the [Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority's online portal](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php); selection runs through periodic mini-lotteries rather than a simple waitlist line, and CMHA's published income limit for a single-person household, effective April 3, 2025, is **$34,800**.

Our current rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast-side neighborhoods rather than directly in Little Italy, so we can't promise a specific voucher-friendly home in this exact district today. Every home we manage accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready — see our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full process, or [tell us your budget](/p/book-showing) so we can point you to comparable current availability.

faq.q: Are there cheap houses for rent in Little Italy?
faq.a: Genuinely low-cost houses directly in Little Italy are rare, since the district is small and sits right against University Circle, which keeps demand and prices closer to Cleveland's citywide averages. Renters on a tight budget typically find better prices a few miles out, in neighborhoods like Hough or Glenville.

faq.q: What's a typical cheap rent in Cleveland right now?
faq.a: Citywide, Zumper's July 2026 report puts Cleveland's overall median rent at $1,250 a month, while RentCafe's professionally managed apartment average runs higher at $1,564. Rent Finder Cleveland's own portfolio of rental homes typically runs from about $700 to $1,800, with most around $1,000.

faq.q: Which neighborhoods near Little Italy have cheaper houses?
faq.a: Hough and Glenville, both a few miles from Little Italy on Cleveland's east side, generally offer lower-cost rental houses than the immediate University Circle/Little Italy core, while remaining within a short transit ride of the same institutions.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland have cheap houses in Little Italy?
faq.a: Our rental homes are concentrated in nearby east-side neighborhoods rather than Little Italy specifically. Every home we manage accepts Section 8 vouchers, so tell our team your budget and we'll point you toward comparable current availability.

faq.q: Can I save money by not owning a car near Little Italy?
faq.a: Yes. The RTA Red Line and the HealthLine bus rapid transit route both serve the University Circle station directly next to Little Italy, making it realistic to skip car ownership for many Downtown and University Circle trips.
